hadiths.chapter: 115. CHAPTER ON SCATTERING GIFTS AND THE WEDDING
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Al-Sakooni from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-
Sadiq {a.s}, who said:  
"Escort your brides at night and serve food in the morning."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.366 • Da’a’im Al-Islam, Vol.2 p.210 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.401 
• Al-Ash’athiyat, Vol.1 p.110 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.418 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.208 • Al-Nawadir 
(Lil-Rawandi), Vol.1 p.40 • Al-Wafi, Vol.21 p.382 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.91 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 
p.266

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 115, H. 4403

hadiths.chapter: 116. CHAPTER ON THE WEDDING FEAST (WALIMAH)
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Musa ibn Bakr from Imam Abu al -Hasan al-Awwal {a.s}, that 
the Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace be upon him and his family, said:  
"There is no feast except on five occasions; at a wedding, at childbirth (nifas), at 
circumcision, when a man buys a house, and upon returning from Hajj."  
The wedding refers to marriage, childbirth refers to the postnatal period, circumcision 
refers to the act of circumcision, buying a house refers to acquiring a new home, and 
returning from Hajj refers to returning from pilgrimage to Mecca.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.402 • Al-Khisal, Vol.1 p.313 • Ma’ani Al -Akhbar, Vol.1 
p.272 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.95 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.73 p.157 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.96 p.384

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 116, H. 4404

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT A MAN SHOULD DO WHEN HIS
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, said to some of his companions:  
"When your wife is brought to you, take hold of her forelock, face her toward the Qibla, 
and say: 'O Allah {SWT}, I have taken her with Your {SWT} trust, and by Your {SWT} words, I 
have made her lawful for myself. If You {SWT} decree for me from her a child, then make 
him blessed, righteous, and sound, and do not allow Satan to have any share or 
portion in him.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.402

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 117, H. 4405

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.ar:
4407  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إ َّي  ُّوب َّ إَّلْح َّز  َّإر ِ عَّن ْ عَّمْزِو ن ْن ِ عُّي ْمَّان َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ:  
سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ إ َّ ي ُّكْزَّةُّ إَّلْح ِمَّاعُّ ق ِى  سَّاعَّه ٍ مِن َّ إَّلس َّاعَّاب ِ ق َّال مَّزُّ وَّ إَّلْ ي َّوْمِ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّن ْكَّسِف ُّ 
ق ِي هِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ وَّ ق ِي مَّا ن َّي ْن َّ ع ُّزُّوب ِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ْ ي َّع ِي ث َّ إَّلس  َّق َّق ُّ وَّ مِن ْ طُّلُّوعِ إَّلْق َّح ْزِ إ ِلَّى طُّلُّوعِ إَّ لس  َّمْسِ وَّ ق ِى  إَّلزِّي حِ  
إَّلس َّوْذَّإءِ وَّ إَّلْحَّمْزَّإءِ وَّ إَّلص َّق ْزَّإءِ وَّ إَّلز  َّلْز َّلَّه ِ وَّ لَّق َّذْ ن َّ اب َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لَّي ْلَّه ً عِن ْذَّ ن َّعْض ِ ن ِسَّان ِهِ ق َّان ْح َّسَّف َّ  
إَّلْق َّمَّزُّ ق ِى  ي ِلْك َّ إَّلل َّي ْلَّه ِ ق َّلَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ مِن ْهُّ س َّى ْء  ق َّق َّالَّث ْ لَّهُّ ر َّوْج َّي ُّهُّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ِا َّن ِى  إ َّن ْث َّ  وَّ إ ُّمِّى  إ َّ كُّل ُّ هَّذ َّإ لِن ُّع ْ ض ٍ ق َّق َّالَّ 
«هُّ ي َّعَّالَّى ق َّوْماً ق َّق َّال 
۝ وَّ إ ِن ْ ي َّزَّوْإ كِسْق اً مِن َّ إَّلس َّمٰاءِ سٰاق ِطاً ي َّق ُّولُّوإ سَّحٰاب   مَّزْكُّوم  ۝ »ي ْمُّ إَّلل َّهِ لأَّ ي ُّح َّامِغُّ إ َّجَّذ  ق ِى  هَّذ ِةِ إَّلس َّاعَّاب ِ 
إَّل َّي ِى  وَّصَّق ْث ُّ ق َّي ُّزْر َّق َّ مِن ْ ج ِمَّاعِهِ وَّلَّذإً وَّ ق َّذْ سَّمِغَّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلْحَّذِي ث َّ ق َّي َّزَّى مَّا ي ُّحِث
hadiths.en:
Sulaiman bin Ja'far al-Ja'fari narrated from Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar 
Al-Kadhim {a.s}, that he said: "Whoever approaches his wife during the waning phase of 
the moon, let him be prepared for the miscarriage of the child."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.499 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.402 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.411 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.716 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.127 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.326 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4406
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.ar:
4408  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن  َّهُّ مَّن ْ ق َّعَّلَّ ذ َّلِك َّ  
ق َّلْي ُّسَّلِّمْ لِسِق ْظِ إَّلْوَّلَّذِ ق َّا ِن ْ ي َّم َّ إ َّوْس َّك َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون َّ مَّح ْن ُّون اً إ َّ لأَّ ي َّزَّى  إ َّن  َّ إَّلْمَّح ْن ُّون َّ إ َّكْي َّزُّ مَّا ي ُّصْزَّعُّ ق ِى  إ َّو َّلِ إَّلس  َّهْزِ وَّ وَّسَّطِهِ 
وَّ إ ج ِزِةِ
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan bin Mahbub narrated from Abu Ayyub al -Khazzaz, from Amr bin 
Uthman, from Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said:  
I asked Imam {a.s}: "Is it disliked to have intercourse at certain times?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: “Yes, it is disliked during a night when the moon is eclipsed, during 
the day when the sun is eclipsed, between sunset and the disappearance of twilight, 
from the rising of dawn until the rising of the sun, during black, red, and yellow winds, 
and during earthquakes." 
Imam {a.s} continued: "The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace be upon him and his family, 
spent a night with one of his wives, and the moon was eclipsed that night. He {saws} 
refrained from intimacy.  
His {saws} wife said to him: 'O Messenger of Allah {SWT}, may my father and mother be 
sacrificed for you, is all of this due to dislike?"  
He {saws} replied: "Woe to you! This celestial event has occurred, and I disliked to take 
pleasure and engage in something (worldly) at such a time. Indeed, Allah, the Exalted, 
has reproached a people, saying: ‘And if they see a piece of the sky falling, they would 
say, "A heap of clouds!"’  (Surah At-Tur 52:44) 
By Allah {SWT}, no one engages in intimacy during these times that I {saws} have described, 
having heard this explanation, and is granted a child from that intimacy, except that 
they will see what they dislike." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.403 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.411 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.20 p.126 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4407
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, said:  
"Do not have intercourse at the beginning of the month, in its middle, or at its end. For 
whoever does so should prepare himself for the possibility of miscarriage.  
And if the child is born, it is likely to suffer from mental instability.  
Do you not see that most cases of insanity occur at the beginning, middle, and end of 
the month?"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.403 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.212 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 
p.718 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.129 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.14 p.227 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4408

reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.ar:
4410   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْق َّي ْض ِ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق َّق َّالَّ إ ُّج َّامِغُّ وَّ إ َّن َّا عُّزْي َّان   ق َّال 
وَّ لأَّ ي َّسْي َّذْن ِزُّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, said: "It is disliked to engage in 
janabah (ritual impurity due to sexual intercourse) when the sun is turning yellow and 
when it rises while it is yellow."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.1 p.84 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.404 • Makarim 
Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.212 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.718 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.730 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.139 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4409

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn al-Faydh asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-
Sadiq {a.s}, saying: "May I engage in intercourse while being completely naked?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: “No, and do not face the Qibla nor turn your back to it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.404 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4410

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.ar:
4412  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ و إ لِهِ
إِجْي ِلاَّمِهِ إَّل َّذ ِى  رَّإ َّى ق َّا ِن ْ ق َّعَّلَّ ق َّح َّزَّح َّ إَّلْوَّلَّذُّ مَّح ْن ُّون اً ق َّلاَّ ي َّلُّومَّن  َّ إ ِلأ َّ ن َّق ْسَّه
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Do not engage in intercourse on a ship."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.404 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.727 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.20 
p.138 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4411

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.ar:
4413  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ مَّح ْذ ُّوماً إ َّوْ إ َّن ْزَّضَّ  
ق َّلاَّ ي َّلُّومَّن  َّ إ ِلأ َّ ن َّق ْسَّه
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
said: "It is disliked for a man to approach his wife after having experienced a nocturnal 
emission (wet dream) until he has performed ghusl (ritual purification) from that 
emission. If he does so and the ch ild is born with a mental disorder, he should blame 
none but himself."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.404 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.212 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 
p.729 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.139 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.139 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4412

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON THE TIMES WHEN INTERCOURSE IS
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
said: "Whoever has intercourse with his wife while she is menstruating, and a child is 
born afflicted with leprosy or vitiligo, let him blame none but himself."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.1 p.96 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.404 • Makarim 
Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.212 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.307 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.719 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.2 p.318

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 118, H. 4413

hadiths.chapter: 119. CHAPTER ON SAYING THE NAME OF ALLAH
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, said:  
"If one of you approaches his wife, let him remember Allah {SWT}, for whoever does not 
remember Allah {SWT} during intercourse, and a child is born from it, that child will have 
a share of Satan's partnership.  
This can be recognized by the child's love or hatred for us."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.404 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.711 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.20 
p.137 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.60 p.201

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 119, H. 4414

hadiths.chapter: 120. CHAPTER ON THE PERMISSIBLE DURATION FOR
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya asked Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar -Ridha {a.s}, about a man who 
has a young wife and refrains from approaching her for months or even a year, not 
intending to harm her but due to some hardship or difficulty they are facing. Is he 
considered sinful for this?  
The Imam {a.s} replied: “If he refrains from her for four months, he is sinful after that 
unless it is with her consent."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 3 p.405 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 7 p.412 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Vol.7 p.419 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.140

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.ar:
4417 - رَّوَّى ذَّإوُّذُّ ن ْن ُّ سِزْجَّان َّ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّل ل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ:   
۝  إَّلز ّٰإن ِى  لأٰ ي َّن ْكِحُّ إ ِلأّٰ ر ٰإن ِي َّه ً إ َّوْ مُّس ْزِكَّه ً وَّ إَّلز ّٰإن ِي َّه ُّ لأٰ ي َّن ْكِحُّهٰا إ ِلأّٰ ر ٰإن ٍ إ َّوْ مُّس ْزِك    ۝  ق َّال 
وَّ رِج َّال  مَّس ْهُّورُّون َّ ن ِالز ِّن َّا س ُّهِزُّوإ ن ِالز ِّن َّا وَّ عُّزِق ُّوإ ن ِهِ وَّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ إَّلْي َّ وْمَّ ن ِي ِلْك َّ إَّلْمَّن ْز ِلَّه ِ مَّن ْ إ ُّق ِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ جَّذ ُّ إَّلز ِّن َّا إ َّوْ س ُّهِزَّ 
ن ِالز ِّن َّا لَّمْ ي َّن ْن َّغ ِ لِأ َّجَّذٍ إ َّن ْ ي ُّن َّاكِحَّهُّ جَّي  َّى ي َّعْزِف َّ مِن ْهُّ ي َّوْن َّه ً
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abu al-Maghra from Al-Halabi who said that Abu Abdullah 
{a.s}, said: “A woman who openly commits adultery should not be married, nor should 
a man who openly commits adultery be given in marriage, unless repentance is known 
from both of them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.405 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 p.168 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 
p.204 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.438 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4416
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢١، ح ٤٤١٦

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Dawood bin Sirhan from Zurarah, who said that Abu Abdullah 
{a.s}, was asked about the saying of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic:  
"The adulterer does not marry except an adulteress or a polytheist, and the adulteress 
does not marry except an adulterer or a polytheist."  (Surah An-Nur 24:3) 
Imam {a.s} said: “They are women known for adultery and men known for adultery, who 
have been publicly infamous for committing adultery and are recognized for it.  
Today, people in such a situation, whether the prescribed punishment for adultery has 
been carried out on them or they are publicly known for adultery, should not be 
married until their repentance is established."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.354 • Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.354 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.405 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.439 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.4 p.46 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.4 p.46 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.101 
p.11 • Tafsir Nur Al -Thaqalayn, Vol.3 p.571 • Tafsir Nur Al -Thaqalayn, Vol.3 p.572 • Tafsir Kanz Al -Daqaiq, 
Vol.9 p.244

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4417
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.ar:
4419  -  ورَّوَّى جَّق ْضُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْن َّح ْي َّزِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ ِسْحَّاق َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ي ُّ زِي ذُّ ي َّز ْوِي ح َّ  
إِمْزَّإ َّة ٍ ق َّذْ طُّلِّق َّث ْ ي َّلاَّي اً كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ق ِي هَّا ق َّال ى  ر َّوْج َّهَّا وَّ مَّعَّهُّ رَّج ُّلاَّن ِ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ 
لَّهُّ ق َّذْ طَّل َّق ْث َّ ق ُّلاَّن َّه َّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ ق َّالَّ ن َّعَّمْ ي َّزَّكَّهَّا ي َّلاَّي َّه َّ إ َّس ْهُّزٍ ي ُّم َّ ج َّطَّن َّهَّا إ ِلَّى ن َّق ْسِهِ
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Beware of marrying women who have been divorced three 
times in a single sitting, for they are still considered to have spouses."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.406 • Al-Fusul Al-Mukhtarah, Vol.1 p.177 • Tahdhib Al-
Ahkam, Vol.7 p.470 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.8 p.56 • Al -Istibsar, Vol.3 p.289 • Mutashabih Al -Quran wa 
Mukhtalifah, Vol.2 p.195 • Al-Wafi, Vol.21 p.270 • Al-Wafi, Vol.23 p.1061 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.495 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.22 p.68 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4418

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.ar:
4420   -  وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّزَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  لَّكُّمْ لِأ َّن  َّكُّمْ لأَّ ي َّزَّوْن َّ 
إَّلي  َّلاَّب َّ س َّي ْن اً وَّ هُّمْ ي ُّوج ِن ُّون َّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Hafs ibn Al -Bakhtari narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar,  from Abu Abdullah  
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, regarding a man who wishes to marry a 
woman who has been divorced three times.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should leave her until she experiences a menstrual period and 
becomes pure. Then he should go to her husband along with two men and ask him, 
'Have you divorced so-and-so?'  
If he says yes, he should leave her for three months and then propose marriage to her 
himself."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.406 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.470 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.22 p.76 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4419
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢١، ح ٤٤١٩

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.en:
And in another narration, Imam {a.s} said: "Indeed, your triple divorce does not 
become permissible for others, but their divorce becomes permissible for you 
because you do not consider the triple divorce as binding, whereas they deem it 
obligatory."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.406

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4420

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.ar:
4422  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ وَّهْث ٍ وَّ ع َّي ْزِةِ مِن ْ إ َّصْحَّان ِن َّا عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: 
سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِ ي َّي َّز َّو َّح ُّ إَّلْي َّهُّوذِي  َّه َّ وَّ إَّلن  َّصْزَّإن ِي  َّه َّ ق َّق َّال ه َّ ق َّمَّا ي َّصْن َّغُّ ن ِا لْي َّهُّوذِي  َّه ِ وَّ إَّلن  َّصْزَّإن ِي  َّه ِ 
»ي زِ وَّ إِعْلَّمْ إ َّن  َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق ِى 
ذِي ن ِهِ ق ِى  ي َّز ْوِي ح ِهِ إ ِي  َّاهَّا ع َّص َّاص َّه ً
hadiths.en:
And Imam {a.s} said: "Whoever follows the religion of a people is bound by their 
rulings."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V.3 p.407 • Al-Wafi, V.21 p.294 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, V.22 p.74 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4421

hadiths.chapter: 121. CHAPTER ON WHAT ALLAH
hadiths.ar:
4423  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ ن ْن ِ رَّر ِي ن ٍ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ:  
سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إَّلْمُّسْلِمِ ي َّي َّز َّو َّح ُّ إَّلْمَّح ُّوسِي  َّه َّ ق َّق َّال ه   ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّطَّا َّهَّا وَّ ي َّعْز ِلَّ 
عَّن ْهَّا وَّ لأَّ ي َّطْلُّث َّ وَّلَّذَّهَّا
hadiths.en:
And Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Muawiyah ibn Wahb and others of 
our companions, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}.  
He said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a believing man marrying a Jewish or Christian woman.  
Imam {a.s} replied: “If he has access to a Muslim woman, what need does he have for a 
Jewish or Christian woman?"  
I said: “He may have affection for her."  
Imam {a.s} said: “If he does so, then he must prevent her from drinking wine and eating 
pork, and know that there is a deficiency in his religion for marrying her."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.407 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 121, H. 4422
